title: dentaria californica var. cuneata
intro: Dentaria californica var. cuneata, also known as the cut-leaved toothwort, is a perennial plant native to the western United States. It belongs to the mustard family and is known for its unique, toothed leaves and white flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The leaves and roots of the cut-leaved toothwort can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion.
